commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Blasphemy. The enemy insults over us (Calmet) and over God. (Haydock)
Birth. Hebrew, "the mouth of the womb. "(Vatable)
This comparison shows the utmost distress to which the people of Jerusalem were reduced. Any great anguish is denoted by a woman in travail, Deuteronomy ii. 25., and Psalm xlvii. 7. Homer (Iliad A) thus describes the uneasiness of Agamemnon. (Calmet)
Ezechias found himself unable to contend with the Assyrian, though he wished to do it. (Menochius)
Without courage, all hope of escaping is lost. (Du Hamel)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
